comment_text.sn1.35:1.2: For the deities to stand in the air is disrespectful if not downright threatening (DN 3:1.21.1.).
comment_text.sn1.35:6.4: The texts usually mention the Buddha smiling when he arrives at some place of special, hidden significance (MN 81:2.1, MN 83:2.1, AN 5.180:1.3). However, at Thag 12.2:11.1 he smiled when Sunīta, having gone forth from a low-born family, was honored by the gods. In both cases the Buddha smiled at the point when the gods rightly honored one who had been seen as contemptible.
comment_text.sn1.35:8.3: Commentary glosses yadi verāni na sammeyyuṃ, suggesting a reading na ce, weakened for the metre.

root_text.sn1.35:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn1.35:1.2: Atha kho sambahulā ujjhānasaññikā devatāyo abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā vehāsaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu.
root_text.sn1.35:1.3: Vehāsaṁ ṭhitā kho ekā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.35:3.2: yaṁ na kayirā na taṁ vade;
root_text.sn1.35:6.1: Atha kho tā devatāyo pathaviyaṁ patiṭṭhahitvā bhagavato pādesu sirasā nipatitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn1.35:6.2: “accayo no, bhante, accagamā yathābālaṁ yathāmūḷhaṁ yathāakusalaṁ, yā mayaṁ bhagavantaṁ āsādetabbaṁ amaññimhā.
root_text.sn1.35:6.3: Tāsaṁ no, bhante, bhagavā accayaṁ accayato paṭiggaṇhātu āyatiṁ saṁvarāyā”ti.
root_text.sn1.35:6.5: Atha kho tā devatāyo bhiyyoso mattāya ujjhāyantiyo vehāsaṁ abbhuggañchuṁ.
root_text.sn1.35:6.6: Ekā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:

translation_text.sn1.35:0.2: 4. The Host of a Hundred Felicities
translation_text.sn1.35:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Monastery.
translation_text.sn1.35:1.2: Then, late at night, several glorious deities of the host of the fault-finders, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the Buddha, and stood in the air.
translation_text.sn1.35:1.3: Standing in the air, one deity spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.35:2.2: to be other than they really are,
translation_text.sn1.35:2.4: who enjoys what was gained by theft.
translation_text.sn1.35:3.1: You should only say what you would do;
translation_text.sn1.35:3.2: you shouldn’t say what you wouldn’t do.
translation_text.sn1.35:4.5: by which the attentive practicing absorption
translation_text.sn1.35:5.1: The attentive certainly don’t act like that,
translation_text.sn1.35:5.2: for they understand the way of the world.
translation_text.sn1.35:5.3: The attentive are quenched by understanding,
translation_text.sn1.35:5.4: they’ve crossed over clinging to the world.”
translation_text.sn1.35:6.1: Then those deities landed on the ground, bowed with their heads at the Buddha’s feet and said,
translation_text.sn1.35:6.2: “We made a mistake, sir. It was foolish, stupid, and unskillful of us to presume to attack the Buddha!
translation_text.sn1.35:6.3: Please, sir, accept our mistake for what it is, so we will restrain ourselves in future.”
translation_text.sn1.35:6.5: Then those deities, becoming even more fault-finding, flew up in the air.
translation_text.sn1.35:6.6: One deity spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.35:7.3: with hidden anger and heavy hate,
translation_text.sn1.35:8.2: and no-one here had gone astray:
translation_text.sn1.35:10.4: and that’s who doesn’t go astray.
translation_text.sn1.35:11.1: If you don’t give your pardon
translation_text.sn1.35:11.3: with hidden anger and heavy hate,
translation_text.sn1.35:11.5: I don’t approve of such enmity,
translation_text.sn1.35:11.6: and so I pardon your mistake.”

variant_text.sn1.35:6.2: yathābālaṁ yathāmūḷhaṁ yathāakusalaṁ → yathābālā yathāmūḷhā yathāakusalā (sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)
variant_text.sn1.35:8.2: nocidhāpagataṁ → nocīdha apahataṁ (sya-all, km); nocīdhāpagataṁ (pts1ed); no cidhāpagataṁ (pts2ed); nocidhāpakataṁ (?)
variant_text.sn1.35:10.6: sova → sodha (bj, sya-all, km); so ca (pts1ed)
